Notice Of Motion To Modify Order Granting Defendant Jpmorgan Chases Motion For Order Of Reference Regarding Paying Party And Agreed Upon Referee, Or Alternatively, To Vacate Order
Off Calendar Dec-01-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Mia Blackler, 415.981.0550 of Lubin Olson & Niewiasomski LLP appearing via zoom for Defendant JP Morgan Chase Bank, N.A.
Karen Uchiyama, 415-563-9300 of the Law Offices of Karen Y. Uchiyama appearing for Plaintiff Nozari 2, LLC.

Hearing held. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFFS' NOTICE OF MOTION TO MODIFY ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE'S MOTION FOR ORDER OF REFERENCE REGARDING PAYING PARTY AND AGREED UPON REFEREE, OR ALTERNATIVELY, TO VACATE ORDER is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion to Modify the Order regarding the agreed upon referee is GRANTED. Judge Lynn O'Malley-Tailor shall be the referee is she is still available to conduct the proceedings. Defendant to present an amended ADR-110 fir execution no later than December 5, 2022. If judge O'Malley-Taylor is no longer available, parties shall either (1) provide a joint declaration no later than December 5, 2022 regarding an agreed upon new referee along with an amended ADR-110; or (2) each submit a declaration providing 3 names of proposed referees no later than December 5, 2022.
Motion is otherwise denied. Issues regarding adhesion, payment of fees, unconscionability, etc., were to be addressed and were necessarily decided on the motion for reference.
The Court notes that the opening brief was 15 pages long and contained to Table of Contents or Table of Authorities, therefore, the last 5 pages of the brief are considered and stricken. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)
